# Orchesis

> Open-source AI agent security proxy. Transparent HTTP proxy
> between AI agents and LLM APIs. 17-phase detection pipeline.
> Zero code changes required. MIT license.

## Key pages

- [Homepage](https://orchesis.ai/): Overview of Orchesis proxy
- [OpenClaw Integration](https://orchesis.ai/openclaw): Security for OpenClaw agents
- [Paperclip Integration](https://orchesis.ai/paperclip): Security for Paperclip AI fleets
- [MCP Security Scanner](https://orchesis.ai/scan): Free browser-based scanner, 52 checks
- [Security Scorecard](https://orchesis.ai/scorecard): AI agent security assessment
- [Blog](https://orchesis.ai/blog): Research and incident analysis

## Key facts

- pip install orchesis
- 4,549 passing tests
- 33+ detection patterns across 10 categories
- 96% explicit injection detection, 0 false positives
- Loop detection at API call #3 ($0.05 vs $55-150)
- Works with: OpenClaw, Paperclip, CrewAI, LangChain, AutoGen, Google ADK, Claude Code, Cursor
- Integration: one line change (base_url = "http://localhost:8080/v1")
- License: MIT
- GitHub: github.com/poushwell/orchesis

## Research

- 15+ formal impossibility results from 8 branches of mathematics
- Proves architectural limits of SDK-based vs proxy-based AI agent security
- Self-Detection Impossibility: compromised agent cannot detect own compromise
- Fleet Metric Impossibility: SDK cannot compute fleet metrics without O(n²) overhead
- Causal Graph Impossibility: single-agent trace cannot reconstruct cross-agent causality
- Cascading Injection Theorem: one poisoned task infects 13 agents in 10 minutes
- File-Based Collusion Impossibility: filesystem collusion invisible to any HTTP proxy

## Blog posts

- [We scanned 900 MCP configs. 75% had problems.](https://orchesis.ai/blog/mcp-scan)
- [An AI agent compromised 7 repos in one week.](https://orchesis.ai/blog/hackerbot-claw)
- [Why your AI agent can't detect its own compromise.](https://orchesis.ai/blog/proxy-vs-decorator)
